method: vendored llama flash_attn_tile<128,128,1,4,false> + flash_attn_combine_results<128> from the on-disk gfx1100 .co, launched via tinygrad HCQ (NamedAMDProgram); kernarg captured from a real ggml-hip decode (LD_PRELOAD shim) and replayed with VAs patched to tinygrad Buffers + COV5 hidden-arg block populated.
timing_note: GPU-busy time (signal timestamps / ProfileGraphEvent) is the kernel-win authority; wall is launch-overhead-bound (llama = 2 raw HCQ dispatches/call, coop = 1 jit graph) and NOT the gate.
derisk_verdict: GPU-KERNEL WIN CONFIRMED (>=1.5x): the vendored llama tile is faster GPU-time when dispatched by tinygrad's HCQ
b2_caveat: CRITICAL for B2 (W==D): by WALL time the 2-raw-HCQ-dispatch path (tile+combine) is ~2.54x SLOWER than the single jit comparator -- the GPU-time win is eaten by per-call launch overhead. A W==D win REQUIRES graph-integrating the launches (folding tile+combine into the model JIT graph / one dispatch), else the kernel advantage does not transfer. This is the recurring 'isolated wins don't transfer to in-model integration' finding.
promotion_policy: NON-PROMOTABLE — vendored llama reference, never a default route

comparator.why_current_winner: shipped default decode-attention primitive (gqa_coop_vec); the local-A/B winner the vendored llama oracle must beat to justify integrating the escape hatch

harness_contract.timing_authority: LOCAL GPU-launch throughput proxy (back-to-back perf_counter, median-of-5, clock-pinned) -- DIAGNOSTIC, NOT in-model W==D; vendored reference, never promotable
